title: Cost-effectiveness analysis of human–AI collaboration in disease screening
data_sources: Lifeline Express diabetic retinopathy screening programme (China)

data_scope.case_study: real-world diabetic retinopathy screening programme in China (Lifeline Express)
data_scope.images_in_filtering_step: 72,044 sent to human review + 594,011 retained negative (~666,055 total)
data_scope.headline_result: annual copilot saves US$1.32M, gains 14.73 QALYs, net monetary benefit US$1.88M per 100,000
data_scope.data_availability: original programme data not public; available on request to corresponding author

core_conclusion: Across 8 AI-based screening strategies and 270 human–AI collaboration scenarios modelled on a real-world diabetic retinopathy programme in China, a 'copilot human–AI' strategy (AI triage + human review of filtered cases) is most cost-effective. An annual copilot strategy across all ages would save US$1.32 million while gaining 14.73 QALYs over a lifetime — net monetary benefit US$1.88 million per 100,000 population. Human involvement is indispensable to maximize clinical benefit and cost-effectiveness; fully autonomous AI is dominated in the reference setting.
why_it_matters: Reframes AI deployment from replacing graders to collaborating with them, putting a price on human judgement; grounded in an operational screening programme; provides concrete sensitivity thresholds (e.g., AI specificity >96.9% favours AI-only, secondary-grader specificity <88.7% favours manual) for policy decisions.
why_nature: Generalizable 'indispensable human' principle for AI in medicine; decision-grade health-economic rigour (Markov microsimulation, ICERs, WTP, tornado + probabilistic sensitivity); directly policy-translatable to screening-frequency and staffing decisions; timely contribution to the human-AI collaboration debate.
added_value_to_my_research: Geography-conditioned cost-effectiveness surface linking copilot value to 2SFCA/R2SFCA spatial accessibility tiers
Subgroup-specific AI error rates + explainable ML to audit equity of an AI screening copilot across regions
Replace fixed Markov transition rates with patient-level digital twins and spatially explicit agent-based deployment
Reusable health-economic evaluation template ('is the human still worth it here?') for AI public-health services
Multi-objective efficiency-vs-equity optimization of copilot thresholds, reusing my EV-charging allocation machinery
